    "R1.1",

    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which primary direction should the strengthening focus on: (A) prose clarity and tone (R1.6), (B) tightening logic and aligning claims with reported evidence (R1.2/R1.3), or (C) reframing the contribution and novelty positioning, potentially adding citations (R1.1/R1.4/R1.7)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening may require sharpening the core claim and framing of QMaster to make the contribution clearer and more compelling.",
    "R1.2": "A stronger piece must ensure the method description and claims are logically consistent and non-contradictory.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about performance and sample efficiency should align tightly with the reported evaluations to bolster credibility.",
    "R1.6": "Improving sentence-level clarity, concision, and tone typically makes the text feel stronger and more persuasive.",
    "RX.1": "Any projection should note uncertainties or missing details that affect how strengthening could proceed.",
    "RX.2": "Avoiding overstatement of performance or novelty is essential when advising on how to make the piece stronger.",
    "RX.3": "The response must stay within responsibility projection and not perform edits or content changes.",
    "RX.4": "Care is needed to avoid encouraging misleading or unsafe claims in robotics contexts.",
    "RX.5": "Responsibilities should be traceable to the specific parts of the delegation and artifact to maintain provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which primary direction should the strengthening focus on: (A) prose clarity and tone (R1.6), (B) tightening logic and aligning claims with reported evidence (R1.2/R1.3), or (C) reframing the contribution and novelty positioning, potentially adding citations (R1.1/R1.4/R1.7)?

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening may require sharpening the core claim and framing of QMaster to make the contribution clearer and more compelling.
rationale.R1.2: A stronger piece must ensure the method description and claims are logically consistent and non-contradictory.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about performance and sample efficiency should align tightly with the reported evaluations to bolster credibility.
rationale.R1.6: Improving sentence-level clarity, concision, and tone typically makes the text feel stronger and more persuasive.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection should note uncertainties or missing details that affect how strengthening could proceed.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overstatement of performance or novelty is essential when advising on how to make the piece stronger.
rationale.RX.3: The response must stay within responsibility projection and not perform edits or content changes.
rationale.RX.4: Care is needed to avoid encouraging misleading or unsafe claims in robotics contexts.
rationale.RX.5: Responsibilities should be traceable to the specific parts of the delegation and artifact to maintain provenance.
